God arranges things so that all praise for victory goes entirely to himself, by choosing only three hundred men to do the work. Being active and careful goes along with depending on God for help in our lawful efforts. When the Lord sees that people are about to ignore him, or because they don't believe, avoid dangerous tasks, or boast against him out of pride, he will set them aside and do his work through others. Many people will find excuses for abandoning the cause and escaping hardship. But even if a religious group becomes smaller in number this way, it will gain in purity and may expect an increased blessing from the Lord. God chooses to use those who are not only well-disposed but also zealously devoted to what is good. They did not resent the freedom given to those who were dismissed. When doing duties required by God, we must not consider the eagerness or reluctance of others, nor what they do, but what God expects from us. It is rare for someone to tolerate others excelling them in gifts or blessings, or in freedom; so we can say that it is by God’s special grace that we listen to what God says to us and don't look to people what they do.

The dream seemed to have little meaning at first, but the interpretation clearly showed that it came from the Lord and revealed that the name of Gideon had filled the Midianites with terror. Gideon took this as a certain sign of success; he immediately worshipped and praised God, and returned confidently to his three hundred men. Wherever we are, we may speak to God and worship him. God must receive praise for what strengthens our faith. And his providence must be acknowledged in events, even if they seem small or accidental.

This method of defeating the Midianites may be referred to as an example of destroying the devil’s kingdom in the world through preaching the everlasting gospel, sounding that trumpet, and shining light from humble vessels, for such are the ministers of the gospel. God chose what is foolish in the world to shame the wise; a barley cake overthrew the tents of Midian so that the power might be seen as coming only from God. The gospel is a sword, not held in the hand, but spoken with the mouth: the sword of the Lord and of Gideon; of God and Jesus Christ, of Him who sits on the throne and the Lamb. Wicked people are often led to avenge God’s cause against each other under the influence of their delusions and the fury of their passions. See also how God frequently makes the enemies of the church instruments to destroy one another; it is regrettable if the church's friends act like them.

Two chief commanders of the Midianite army were captured and killed by the men of Ephraim. It would be desirable if we all acted as they did, and that when help is needed, it is willingly and readily provided by others. And if there is any excellent and useful work begun, we are willing to have fellow workers to finish and perfect it, and not, as often happens, hinder one another.
